description: Translation & Immersion Pipeline — i18n framework with CEFR-aware locale switching, quest translation fields, translation caching, hover-translate vocabulary tracking, pre-generated translations, CEFR-aware quest display, action prompt translation, progressive notification translation, UI immersion settings, dynamic LLM translation generation, Babylon.js in-world text translation, and automated tests

userStories.title: Add i18n framework with CEFR-aware locale switching
userStories.description: As a player, I need game UI strings to be translatable with CEFR-aware switching so that interface elements progressively transition to the target language as my proficiency grows.
acceptanceCriteria: Install i18next + react-i18next + i18next-browser-languagedetector
Create i18n initialization at client/src/i18n/index.ts: two locale namespaces (en + dynamic target), English fallback, interpolation escaping

Create client/src/i18n/locales/en/common.json with ALL extractable UI strings organized by domain (ui, actions, notifications, menus) — extract from shared/game-engine/rendering/*.ts, client/src/components/*.tsx, client/src/pages/*.tsx
Create client/src/i18n/locales/fr/common.json as template target-language file with translations for all keys

Create CEFR-aware immersion controller at shared/language/ui-localization.ts: getUIImmersionLevel(cefrLevel) returns 0-100%, shouldTranslateUIKey(key, cefrLevel) with immersion levels A1=0%, A2=0%, B1=10%, B2=30%, C1=60%, C2=90%
Priority: actions namespace first (B2), then ui (C1), then notifications (C1), then menus (C2)
Create useGameTranslation(key) React hook wrapping useTranslation() with CEFR immersion logic
Wire i18n into app entry point; set target language from world's targetLanguage field
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: All UI text is hardcoded English. No i18n framework exists.

userStories.title: Add translation fields to quest schema
userStories.description: As a player at lower CEFR levels, I need English translations of quest titles, descriptions, and objectives so that I can understand my quest goals.
acceptanceCriteria: Add titleTranslation (string, nullable), descriptionTranslation (string, nullable), objectivesTranslation (string[], nullable) to quest schema in shared/schema.ts
Create MongoDB migration with null defaults
Update Mongoose schema in mongo-storage.ts with new fields
Update quest generation LLM prompts to generate both target-language AND English; parse and store both
Update quest display with CEFR logic: A1-A2 English with target-language subtitle, B1 target-language with hover-for-English, B2+ target-language only
Quest objectives use same parallel translation display logic
Add backfill batch translation endpoint for legacy quests (similar to batchTranslateItems pattern)
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Quests have target-language title/description but NO English translation fields, unlike GameText.

userStories.title: Implement translation caching with per-world dictionary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need word translations cached per-world so that repeated lookups don't hit the LLM and players get consistent translations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create wordTranslationCache schema in shared/schema.ts: worldId, sourceWord (indexed), targetLanguage, translation, partOfSpeech, context, createdAt, lookupCount. Compound unique index on (worldId, sourceWord, targetLanguage)
Add Mongoose model to mongo-storage.ts with findTranslation(), upsertTranslation(), incrementLookupCount()
Update translate-word endpoint in http-bridge.ts (line 641): check cache first, return cached + increment count on hit; store after LLM call on miss
Pre-populate cache during world generation from batchTranslateItems() and vocabularyHighlights
Add bulk cache warming endpoint: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/translation-cache/warm with batched LLM translation
Call cache warming during world generation for CEFR-appropriate frequency tier
Add cache statistics to world admin view: total cached, hit rate, most-looked-up
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: translate-word API hits LLM for EVERY request with no caching.

userStories.title: Connect hover-translate to vocabulary tracking
userStories.description: As a player, I need hover-translate lookups to count as vocabulary encounters so that my word exposure is tracked and contributes to mastery.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful translation fetch in HoverTranslationSystem (line 154), call recordVocabularyEncounter() with word, translation, source='hover_translate', timestamp
Add onWordEncounter callback to HoverTranslationSystem constructor; wire in BabylonGame.ts to update LanguageProgress via data source
Hover-translate counts as 'passive_hover' encounter (0.5x weight for mastery vs 'active_use')
Add encounterType field: 'active_use' | 'passive_hover' | 'passive_read' | 'quiz_correct' | 'quiz_incorrect'
Update mastery calculation in progress.ts calculateMasteryLevel() to weight passive encounters at 0.5x
Show encounter count in hover UI: 'Seen 3 times' alongside translation
Add 'words looked up today' counter to progress dashboard filtered by hover_translate source
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: HoverTranslationSystem.ts fetches translations but lookups are NOT connected to vocabulary progress tracking.

userStories.title: Pre-generate common translations during world creation
userStories.description: As a player, I need common vocabulary pre-translated at world creation so that translations are instant and consistent instead of on-the-fly LLM calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/world-translation-generator.ts running during world creation
Pre-generate translations for: CEFR-appropriate frequency vocabulary (A1 top 200, A2 top 500, etc.), all location/building names, NPC names and titles, common game nouns/verbs
Create base vocabulary seed at data/seed/language/base-vocabulary.json structured by language and CEFR level (start with French)
Batch-translate using batchTranslateItems() pattern (batches of 50) and store in wordTranslationCache
Generate bilingual quest versions with both target-language and English stored
Wire into world creation pipeline as final step after settlement/NPC/item generation with progress logging
Add re-generation endpoint: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/regenerate-translations
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Only item names are batch-translated currently. All other translations are on-the-fly.

userStories.title: CEFR-aware quest display
userStories.description: As a player, I need quests displayed bilingually based on my proficiency so that I can understand quest objectives at any CEFR level.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/language/quest-localization.ts with getLocalizedQuestText(quest, cefrLevel, field): A1 English primary + target-language subtitle, A2 target-language + English parenthetical, B1 target-language + hover-to-reveal English, B2+ target-language only
Update quest tracker HUD: A1-A2 English objective with target-language below, B1+ target-language only
Update quest journal/log panel with same logic; A1 gets 'Show in English' toggle button
Update quest acceptance dialogs with getLocalizedQuestText()
Update quest completion notifications with CEFR-appropriate language
Handle missing translations gracefully: fall back to target-language at all levels if titleTranslation is null, log warning
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Uses translation fields from US-002. Quest display is target-language only with no English fallback.

userStories.title: Translate action prompts and building labels
userStories.description: As a player at B2+, I need action prompts and building labels in the target language so that high-frequency contextual UI reinforces vocabulary learning.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify all action prompt strings ([G]: Talk, [E]: Enter, Fish, Examine, Pick Up) and building label rendering locations
Add all action prompts and building labels to i18n translation files (en/common.json and fr/common.json)
Replace hardcoded prompts with i18n lookups: useGameTranslation hook for React, getGameString(key, cefrLevel, targetLanguage) for Babylon.js code
Building labels: B1+ target-language, A1-A2 bilingual (English + target-language in parentheses)
Add hover-to-reveal for translated prompts: hovering or holding Alt shows English equivalent
Start with action prompts as minimum viable; building labels as follow-up if complex
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: Action prompts and building labels are always English regardless of proficiency.

userStories.title: Progressive notification translation at C1+
userStories.description: As an advanced player, I need notifications and system messages transitioning to the target language at C1+ so that immersion is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all notification/toast/system message sources and catalog unique strings
Categorize: 'safe to translate' (quest complete, item received, achievement) vs 'keep in English' (save/load, errors, connection) in separate i18n namespaces
Add safe-to-translate strings under notifications namespace in i18n files
Update notification rendering to use i18n lookups with CEFR gating: only translate notifications at C1+
Achievement descriptions: bilingual at B2, target-language-only at C1+
Daily challenge descriptions: bilingual at B1-B2, target-language-only at C1+
Add translated variants for common events: quest accepted/completed/failed, item acquired, skill improved, area discovered, relationship changed
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: All toasts, notifications, achievement descriptions are English-only.

userStories.title: UI Language Immersion settings and progress indicator
userStories.description: As a player, I need control over UI language immersion level and visibility into my immersion progress so that I can customize my learning experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Language Immersion setting to game settings panel: 'Auto (based on proficiency)' (default), 'English Only', 'Maximum Immersion'
Add uiImmersionMode field to player settings/preferences schema: 'auto' | 'english_only' | 'maximum', default 'auto'
Update getUIImmersionLevel() to respect setting: english_only=0%, maximum=90%, auto=CEFR-based
Add immersion progress indicator to HUD: language icon with percentage, tooltip explaining current state and next CEFR threshold
Add 'What's translated?' info panel from settings listing all UI categories with current state and switch level
One-time notification when immersion level changes due to CEFR advancement
Smooth transition: phase in new translated strings over 5-10 minutes, one category at a time with highlight animation
Typecheck passes, tests pass

userStories.title: Dynamic LLM-generated UI translation files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need UI translation files generated dynamically for any target language so that Insimul supports worlds beyond French.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/services/ui-translation-generator.ts: takes en/common.json, generates complete translation via batched LLM (groups of 50 keys), stores in world data
LLM prompt: 'Translate UI strings to [targetLanguage], keep SHORT (1-3 words), natural, game-appropriate. Return valid JSON same structure.'
Wire into world creation after target language is set; add uiTranslations field to world schema storing full JSON
API endpoint: GET /api/worlds/:worldId/ui-translations; client loads dynamically via i18next backend/custom loader
Handle Louisiana French specifically: LLM prompt includes Kouri-Vini/Louisiana Creole context; add language variant field if needed
Regeneration: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/ui-translations/regenerate
Client caching: localStorage keyed by worldId + targetLanguage, refetch only on version change
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: i18n framework starts with hardcoded French but Insimul supports multiple target languages.

userStories.title: Babylon.js in-world text translation adapting to CEFR
userStories.description: As a player, I need in-world 3D text (signs, labels, name plates) to show bilingual text at lower levels and target-language-only at higher levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all in-world text rendering: TextBlock, DynamicTexture, billboard text in shared/game-engine/rendering/ and client/src/components/3DGame/
Building/shop signs: getLocalizedBuildingSign() — A1-A2 'Boulangerie (Bakery)', B1 target-language + small English subtitle, B2+ target-language only
Street/location names: add English subtitles for lower CEFR if already in target language
Item labels: A1-A2 English, B1 bilingual, B2+ target-language (wire existing item.translations field)
NPC title/role translation (Baker → Boulanger) displayed by CEFR level
Visual distinction for translated text: italic, color accent, or small flag icon
All in-world text updates when CEFR level changes mid-session via CEFR change listener
Typecheck passes, tests pass
userStories.notes: In-world 3D text elements don't adapt to player proficiency level.

userStories.title: Automated tests for translation and immersion pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test suite covering the translation caching, CEFR display logic, and UI immersion to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: ui-localization.test.ts: getUIImmersionLevel() percentages per CEFR, shouldTranslateUIKey() per level/namespace, system keys never translated, three immersion modes
quest-localization.test.ts: getLocalizedQuestText() returns correct format per CEFR, null titleTranslation fallback, objective translation logic
translation-cache.test.ts: cache hit/miss, LLM call skipped on hit, lookup count increment, compound index uniqueness, bulk warming
vocabulary-encounter-tracking.test.ts: hover-translate creates passive_hover encounters, 0.5x weight, accumulation, daily counter
immersion-progression.test.ts: full journey A1 all-English → B1 bilingual labels → B2 translated prompts → C1 translated notifications → C2 full immersion
Edge cases: unsupported language fallback, rapid CEFR advancement, settings toggle mid-session
All tests pass
